summary.headline: Environmental snapshot for New York-Newark-Jersey City — 2 of 10 blocks populated (8 pending upstream migration).
bullets: Coastal metro — tide gauge data available via the optional Coastal Conditions block.
Report is statically generated — see Methodology block for trust-tag vocabulary and source list.

blocks.title: Air Quality
blocks.body: Ambient air quality in the New York-Newark-Jersey City metro is tracked by EPA AirNow (current AQI) and regulated facility compliance via EPA ECHO. Step 5 scaffolding — live AirNow/ECHO data pending v2 connector migration.
blocks.error: Upstream v2 connector not yet wired (Step 5 scaffolding).
notes: AirNow reporting area may differ from the CBSA boundary — regional readings approximate metro conditions.
ECHO compliance status is not a direct measure of local exposure (compliance ≠ exposure).

blocks.title: Climate & Heat
blocks.body: Local climate baseline for New York-Newark-Jersey City is drawn from the NOAA 1991-2020 climate normals at the primary city station. Heat-day counts (days ≥ 90°F / ≥ 95°F) are derived from daily summaries. Step 5 scaffolding — ClimateNormals v2 connector pending.
blocks.error: Upstream v2 connector not yet wired (Step 5 scaffolding).
notes: Reference station: New York JFK Intl AP (USW00094728).

blocks.title: Disaster Exposure
blocks.body: Federal disaster declarations affecting New York County over the past decade are catalogued by OpenFEMA. The summary block reports counts by incident type; the detailed event-level history appears in the optional Disaster History block when ≥1 declaration is present.
blocks.error: Upstream v2 connector not yet wired (Step 5 scaffolding).
notes: Declarations reflect federal response requests, not the full inventory of hazard events.

blocks.title: Drinking Water & Surface Quality
blocks.body: Public water system compliance in New York-Newark-Jersey City is tracked by EPA SDWIS (violations and health-based actions). Ambient surface water quality samples come from the USGS/EPA Water Quality Portal (WQP). Step 5 scaffolding.
blocks.error: Upstream v2 connector not yet wired (Step 5 scaffolding).
notes: WQP samples are discrete observations — date coverage varies by parameter and station.
SDWIS violation counts do not include every small system on identical cadence; reporting latency applies.

blocks.title: Industrial & Emissions
blocks.body: Reporting facilities in and around New York-Newark-Jersey City disclose toxic chemical releases to the EPA Toxics Release Inventory (TRI) and greenhouse-gas emissions to the GHGRP programme. Both inventories lag by roughly one reporting year.
blocks.error: Upstream v2 connector not yet wired (Step 5 scaffolding).
notes: TRI covers threshold facilities only — small emitters are not represented.
GHGRP applies to facilities emitting ≥25,000 metric tons CO₂e.

blocks.title: Contaminated Sites & Cleanup
blocks.body: Federal contaminated-site programmes tracked in New York County include Superfund NPL (National Priorities List), the EPA Brownfields ACRES database, and RCRA hazardous-waste TSDFs.
blocks.error: Upstream v2 connector not yet wired (Step 5 scaffolding).
notes: Site status reflects programme listings — active remediation status and on-site exposure pathways are case-specific.

blocks.title: Population Exposure & Environmental Justice
blocks.body: Roughly 19,768,000 residents live in the New York-Newark-Jersey City CBSA. Full environmental-justice indicators (EJSCREEN percentile exposure + demographic overlap) are a Step-6 deliverable — this Step 5 block reports CBSA-level population only.
notes: CBSA boundaries approximate commuting patterns, not exposure watersheds. Block-group EJ analysis requires a different geographic unit.

blocks.title: Methodology & Data Trust
blocks.body: This report is assembled at build time from multiple federal environmental data programmes. Each block above carries its own `trustTag` indicating how direct the underlying signal is — `observed` (direct measurement), `near-real-time` (satellite or sensor feeds with hours-to-day latency), `forecast` (modelled projections), `compliance` (regulatory self-reporting), and `derived` (composite or estimated quantities). Missing data is reported explicitly, never filled by imputation.
notes: No composite environmental score is computed. Each indicator is reported on its own axis with its source programme named.

blocks.title: Coastal Conditions
blocks.body: Tide gauge observations and mean sea-level trends for New York-Newark-Jersey City are drawn from NOAA CO-OPS. Step 5 scaffolding — CO-OPS v2 connector pending.
blocks.error: Upstream v2 connector not yet wired (Step 5 scaffolding).
notes: Station coverage is non-uniform — the nearest gauge may be outside the CBSA boundary.

blocks.title: Related Metros
blocks.body: Peer metros for comparison — selected on shared climate zone (Humid subtropical (Cfa)) and region.
